record.field: Invite a new user to the platform

Sends an invitation email to a new user. Creates a pending user account with a secure invite token (UUID v4 → SHA-256).
**Authorization Matrix:** - Owner → all 9 profiles - Manager → 5 operational profiles (agent, prospector, receptionist, financial, inspector) - Agent → owner + portal only
**Required Headers:** - `Authorization: Bearer {jwt_token}` - `X-Openerp-Session-Id: {session_id}` - `X-Company-Id: {company_id}`
**Required Fields:** - `name`: Full name of the user (string) - `email`: Email address (RFC 5322 validated, string) - `profile`: RBAC profile (one of: owner, manager, agent, prospector, receptionist, financial, inspector, maintenance, portal)
**Optional Fields (portal profile only):** - `cpf`: Brazilian CPF number (validated, string) - `phone`: Phone number (string)
**Request Example:** ```json { "name": "João Silva", "email": "joao.silva@example.com", "profile": "agent" } ```
**Response (201 Created):** ```json { "success": true, "data": { "user_id": 42, "email": "joao.silva@example.com", "profile": "agent", "status": "pending", "invite_sent_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z" } } ```
**Error Responses:** - **400 Bad Request**: Invalid email, missing fields, invalid profile, duplicate email, invalid CPF - **403 Forbidden**: Caller not authorized to invite target profile - **409 Conflict**: User with this email already exists
Resend invitation email to a pending user

Resends the invitation email to a user whose status is still pending. Invalidates the previous invite token and generates a new one (last-token-wins policy).
**Authorization:** Same matrix as invite endpoint (owner/manager/agent roles).
**Required Headers:** - `Authorization: Bearer {jwt_token}` - `X-Openerp-Session-Id: {session_id}` - `X-Company-Id: {company_id}`
**URL Path Parameter:** - `id`: User ID (integer)
**Response (200 OK):** ```json { "success": true, "data": { "user_id": 42, "email": "joao.silva@example.com", "invite_resent_at": "2025-01-15T11:00:00Z" } } ```
**Error Responses:** - **404 Not Found**: User not found or not in same company - **409 Conflict**: User is not in pending status (already accepted invite)

Sets a user's initial password using the token received via invitation email. Token must be type `invite`. Activates the user account and invalidates the token and current session after success.
**Security:** - Token format: 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters (UUID v4 hex, no dashes) - Stored as SHA-256 hash — raw token never persisted - Token type is validated (invite tokens cannot be used in reset flow and vice versa) - Session is invalidated after password is set
**Required Fields:** - `token`: Invite token from email link (32 hex chars, string) - `password`: New password (min 8 characters, string) - `password_confirm`: Must match `password` (string)
**Request Example:** ```json { "token": "a1b2c3d4e5f6789012345678abcdef01", "password": "MySecurePassword123!", "password_confirm": "MySecurePassword123!" } ```
**Response (200 OK):** ```json { "success": true, "message": "Senha definida com sucesso." } ```
**Error Responses:** - **400 Bad Request**: `invalidTokenFormat` (token not 32 hex chars), `passwordTooShort`, `passwordMismatch`, `missingFields` - **404 Not Found**: Token not found or wrong type - **410 Gone**: Token already used or expired

Sends a password reset email to the provided address if a matching active user exists. Always returns 200 to prevent email enumeration attacks (anti-enumeration pattern).
**Security:** - Always returns HTTP 200 regardless of whether the email exists - Reset token: UUID v4 → SHA-256 hash, sent in email only once - TTL configurable via Settings (default 1 hour, range 1–720h) - Previous pending reset tokens are invalidated before new one is generated
**Required Fields:** - `email`: Email address to send reset link to (string)
**Request Example:** ```json { "email": "joao.silva@example.com" } ```
**Response (200 OK — always):** ```json { "success": true, "message": "Se este e-mail estiver cadastrado, você receberá as instruções de recuperação." } ```
**Note:** A 200 response does NOT confirm the email exists in the system.
Reset password using reset token from email

Resets a user's password using the token received in the forgot-password email. Token must be type `reset`. Invalidates all active sessions after success.
**Security:** - Token format: 32 lowercase hexadecimal characters (UUID v4 hex, no dashes) - Token type validated (reset tokens cannot be used in set-password flow) - All active sessions are invalidated after successful reset - Token is consumed (marked used) after success
**Required Fields:** - `token`: Reset token from email link (32 hex chars, string) - `password`: New password (min 8 characters, string) - `password_confirm`: Must match `password` (string)
**Request Example:** ```json { "token": "f0e1d2c3b4a5987654321fedcba09876", "password": "NewSecurePassword456!", "password_confirm": "NewSecurePassword456!" } ```
**Response (200 OK):** ```json { "success": true, "message": "Senha redefinida com sucesso." } ```
**Error Responses:** - **400 Bad Request**: `invalidTokenFormat` (token not 32 hex chars), `passwordTooShort`, `passwordMismatch`, `missingFields` - **404 Not Found**: Token not found or wrong type - **410 Gone**: Token already used or expired
